documents.title: Approved Document F: Ventilation - Volume 1: Dwellings

must.text: There must be adequate means of ventilation provided for people in the building
Fixed systems for mechanical ventilation and any associated controls must be commissioned by testing and adjustment

should.text: Anyone using the approved documents should have sufficient knowledge and skills to understand the guidance

may.text: There may be other ways to comply with the requirements than those described in an approved document

key_definitions.definition: A self-contained unit designed for residential occupation by a single household
Ventilation provided by a mechanical system, which may be extract only, supply only, or balanced

exceptions.description: Competent person self-certification schemes may not require building control notification
exceptions.condition: Installer registered with competent person scheme

decision_points: Choice of ventilation strategy (natural, mechanical extract, mechanical supply, balanced)
Selection of background ventilators and intermittent extract fans
summary_bullets: Sets out ventilation requirements for dwellings under Building Regulations 2010
Covers Requirement F1: Means of ventilation and Regulations 39, 42 and 44
Provides guidance on adequate ventilation to protect health and safety
Addresses removal of pollutants including moisture, carbon dioxide and odours
Specifies minimum ventilation rates for different room types
Covers natural ventilation, mechanical extract, and balanced systems
Requires commissioning of mechanical ventilation systems
Includes provisions for background ventilators and trickle vents
Addresses purge ventilation for rapid air changes
Provides guidance on air flow rate testing requirements
References British Standards for ventilation performance

documents.title: Approved Document F: Ventilation - Volume 2: Buildings other than Dwellings
regulated_activities: Design of ventilation systems for non-domestic buildings

must.text: There must be adequate means of ventilation provided for people in the building

decision_points: Air quality requirements based on occupancy and use
summary_bullets: Sets out ventilation requirements for non-domestic buildings under Building Regulations 2010
Covers Requirement F1: Means of ventilation and Regulations 39 and 44
Provides guidance for offices, retail, industrial and other non-domestic uses
Addresses occupant health and safety through adequate air supply
Specifies ventilation rates based on building use and occupancy
Covers both natural and mechanical ventilation approaches
Requires commissioning of ventilation systems
References British Standards for commercial ventilation
Addresses control of indoor air pollutants
Provides guidance on CO2 monitoring where applicable

documents.title: Approved Document B: Fire Safety - Volume 2: Buildings other than Dwellings (2019 edition with amendments)

height_thresholds.condition: Triggers additional fire safety requirements including sprinklers in certain building types
Additional means of escape and firefighting requirements

must.text: The building shall be designed and constructed so that there are appropriate provisions for the early warning of fire, and appropriate means of escape in case of fire
To inhibit the spread of fire within the building, the internal linings shall adequately resist the spread of flame over their surfaces
The building shall be designed and constructed so that, in the event of fire, its stability will be maintained for a reasonable period
The external walls of the building shall adequately resist the spread of fire over the walls and from one building to another
The building shall be designed and constructed so as to provide reasonable facilities to assist firefighters in the protection of life

key_definitions.definition: Classification of buildings by their main use for fire safety purposes
A building or part of a building constructed to prevent the spread of fire to or from another part of the same building or an adjoining building

A stair discharging through a final exit to a place of safety, including any exit passageway between the foot of the stair and the final exit, that is adequately enclosed with fire-resisting construction

summary_bullets: Comprehensive fire safety guidance for non-domestic buildings in England
Covers Requirements B1-B5: Warning/escape, internal linings, structure, external spread, firefighter access
Incorporates amendments through 2029 for post-Grenfell fire safety improvements
Addresses means of escape including travel distances and exit capacity
Specifies fire resistance periods for structural elements
Covers compartmentation and fire stopping requirements
Addresses external wall systems including cladding and insulation
Provides guidance on smoke control systems
Specifies firefighting shaft requirements for taller buildings
Covers fire alarm and detection system requirements
Addresses sprinkler system provisions
Includes detailed diagrams and worked examples
confidence_notes: Document references future 2026 and 2029 amendments which may be consolidated versions

documents.title: Delivering the Golden Thread: Guidance for dutyholders and accountable persons

height_thresholds.condition: Definition of higher-risk building with 2+ residential units

must.text: Those responsible for higher-risk buildings must use the golden thread to inform the safe operation and occupation of the building

should.text: The golden thread should be developed during design, through construction, handover and completion of the building and into occupation

key_definitions.definition: The information that those responsible need to assure themselves and reassure residents and the regulator that they understand and are effectively managing the building so that it is safe
The primary mechanism for demonstrating safe operation and occupation of the building, developed from golden thread information

summary_bullets: Practical guidance from industry on implementing golden thread requirements
Developed by Construction Leadership Council working group with industry experience
Endorsed by Dame Judith Hackitt who originated the golden thread concept
Explains purpose of golden thread for rebuilding trust in higher-risk building safety
Details information requirements during design, construction and occupation phases
Links golden thread to safety case report development
Clarifies golden thread is not a checklist but working information system
Provides guidance for dutyholders and accountable persons
Emphasizes golden thread enables effective building management
Acknowledges guidance will evolve as regime develops
Provides feedback mechanisms for continuous improvement
confidence_notes: Guidance will evolve as regulatory regime develops

height_thresholds.condition: Definition of higher-risk building requiring Gateway 2

height_thresholds.condition: HRB definition triggering BSR fees with 2+ residential units

key_definitions.definition: Buildings with at least 7 storeys or 18m height with 2+ residential units, or hospitals/care homes meeting height threshold

documents.title: The Building Regulations 2010 (SI 2010/2214)

must.text: Building work must comply with requirements in Schedule 1
Plans must be deposited with local authority or building notice given before work begins
Notice must be given of commencement and completion of certain stages of work
Fire safety information must be given to responsible person on completion

may.text: Local authority may dispense with or relax requirements

key_definitions.definition: The erection or extension of a building; material alteration; provision, extension or material alteration of controlled services or fittings
Change to building or part becoming a dwelling, hotel, institution, public building, or other specified uses

decision_points: Building notice vs full plans submission
summary_bullets: Principal statutory instrument governing building control in England and Wales
Sets out procedural requirements for building work approval
Defines building work and material change of use
Establishes Schedule 1 functional requirements (Parts A-S)
Covers submission of building notices and full plans
Requires notification at key construction stages
Establishes completion certificate process
Covers supervision by approved inspectors
Enables competent person self-certification schemes
Includes energy efficiency requirements (Part 6)
Includes water efficiency requirements (Part 7)
Requires fire safety information provision
Covers testing and commissioning requirements
confidence_notes: Regulations frequently amended - check current version on legislation.gov.uk

documents.title: The Building (Higher-Risk Buildings Procedures) (England) Regulations 2023 (SI 2023/909)

must.text: Building control approval required for HRB work or stage of HRB work
Change control applications required for major changes during construction
Golden thread information must be maintained
Mandatory occurrence reporting system must be established
Information handover required on completion

key_definitions.definition: Building work to construct a new higher-risk building
Information about the design, construction and maintenance of a higher-risk building that must be kept
Structural failure, fire spread, or other occurrence meeting criteria for mandatory reporting

summary_bullets: Key statutory instrument implementing Building Safety Act 2022 for construction phase
Establishes BSR as building control authority for higher-risk buildings in England

Covers building control approval for work to existing HRBs
Establishes change control regime during construction
Creates mandatory golden thread requirements
Establishes mandatory occurrence reporting system
Sets out completion certificate process for HRBs
Covers inspection powers and regularisation
Includes review and appeals processes
Addresses insolvency and change of dutyholder situations
Requires information handover on completion

documents.title: London Plan Policy D12(B) Fire Statements - Guidance Sheet

must.text: All development proposals must achieve the highest standards of fire safety

should.text: All major development proposals should be submitted with a Fire Statement

key_definitions.definition: A standalone document which defines the fire safety objectives and performance requirements of a development, and the methods by which these objectives will be provided/satisfied

summary_bullets: GLA guidance on London Plan Policy D12(B) Fire Statements
Draft guidance sheet dated July 2020
Requires major developments to submit Fire Statements
Fire safety must be embedded at earliest design stage
Fire Statement is standalone document defining fire safety objectives
Must evidence safety of occupants and property protection
Must address firefighting access and equipment provision
Should include schedule of relevant plan references
Should feature plan excerpts as standalone document
Must align with all other planning application documentation
Explains planning officer role in assessing Fire Statements
